title: Cultivating mindfulness: effects on well-being
headline: Cultivating mindfulness: effects on well-being
summary: A randomized controlled trial of Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) examined stress & anxiety. The current study offers preliminary evidence that at least one aspect of mindfulness, measured by the Mindful Attention and Awareness Scale (MAAS; K. These results suggest that distinct meditation-based practices can increase mindfulness as measured by the MAAS, which may partly mediate benefits.
keyFindings: The current study offers preliminary evidence that at least one aspect of mindfulness, measured by the Mindful Attention and Awareness Scale (MAAS; K.
These results suggest that distinct meditation-based practices can increase mindfulness as measured by the MAAS, which may partly mediate benefits.
limitations: Pretest, posttest, and 8-week follow-up data were gathered on self-report outcome measures.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR)) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
